title: Earliest Time to Swim Across Rising Water
description:
You are given an n×n grid where grid[r][c] is the time when the cell becomes passable. At time t you can move to 4-direction neighbors with grid value ≤ t. Find the minimum t such that a path exists from (0,0) to (n-1,n-1). Solve by binary searching t and checking reachability.

    

Constraints:
    - 1 ≤ n ≤ 100
explanation:
Intuition
Feasibility increases with time. Binary search the minimal time and use BFS/DFS to test connectivity through cells whose value ≤ mid.

Approach
1. Initial Setup: Set lo=max(grid[0][0], grid[n-1][n-1]) and hi=max grid value
2. Main Algorithm: While lo<hi, mid=(lo+hi)//2; BFS from start through cells ≤ mid
3. Return Result: If reach goal, hi=mid else lo=mid+1

Complexity Analysis
- Time Complexity: O(n^2 log W) where W is value range
- Space Complexity: O(n^2)

Key Insights
- Monotonicity makes binary search valid
- Union-Find or Dijkstra are viable alternatives

Common Pitfalls
- Be careful with initial lo based on endpoints
- Reset visited per feasibility check
hints: BFS through cells with value ≤ mid
